Use of Funds From National Defense Stockpile Transaction Fund To Meet National Defense Stockpile Goals and Specifications in Effect onOctober 1, 1984, , , directed that, no later than , Administrator of General Services, or any Federal officer assuming Administrator’s responsibilities with respect to management of the stockpile, to use all funds authorized and appropriated before , from National Defense Stockpile Transaction Fund to evaluate, test, relocate, upgrade or purchase stockpile materials to meet National Defense Stockpile goals and specifications in effect on . Deposit of Funds Accruing From Naval Petroleum Reserves, , , as amended by , , , authorized the deposit into the National Defense Stockpile Transaction Fund, established under this section, of 30 percent of all money accruing to the United States during fiscal years 1985 and 1986 from lands in the naval petroleum and oil shale reserves.Pub. children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a separate fund to be known as the National Defense Stockpile Transaction Fund (hereinafter in this section referred to as the “fund”).
All moneys received from the sale of materials in the stockpile under paragraphs (5) and (6) of shall be covered into the fund.section 98e(a) of this title1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: The acquisition, maintenance, and disposal of strategic and critical materials under .section 98e(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: Transportation, storage, and other incidental expenses related to such acquisition, maintenance, and disposal.

children.label: C
children.text: Development of current specifications of stockpile materials and the upgrading of existing stockpile materials to meet current specifications (including transportation, when economical, related to such upgrading).

children.label: D
children.text: Encouraging the appropriate conservation of strategic and critical materials.

children.label: E
children.text: Testing and quality studies of stockpile materials.

children.label: F
children.text: Studying future material and mobilization requirements for the stockpile.

children.label: G
children.text: Activities authorized under .section 98h–6 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: improve the quality and availability of materials stockpiled from time to time in the stockpile; and

children.label: ii
children.text: develop new materials for the stockpile.

children.label: I
children.text: Improvement or rehabilitation of facilities, structures, and infrastructure needed to maintain the integrity of stockpile materials.

children.label: J
children.text: Disposal of hazardous materials that are stored in the stockpile and authorized for disposal by law.

children.label: K
children.text: Performance of environmental remediation, restoration, waste management, or compliance activities at locations of the stockpile that are required under a Federal law or are undertaken by the Government under an administrative decision or negotiated agreement.

children.label: L
children.text: Pay of employees of the National Defense Stockpile program.

children.label: M
children.text: Other expenses of the National Defense Stockpile program.
Moneys in the fund shall remain available until expended.

children.heading: Moneys received from sale of materials being rotated or disposed of
children.label: c Moneys received from sale of materials being rotated or disposed of
children.text: All moneys received from the sale of materials being rotated under the provisions of or disposed of under shall be covered into the fund and shall be available only for the acquisition of replacement materials.section 98e(a)(4) of this titlesection 98f(a) of this title
be applied toward the total value of materials that are authorized to be disposed of from the stockpile during that fiscal year;

be treated as an acquisition for purposes of satisfying any requirement imposed on the National Defense Stockpile Manager to enter into obligations during that fiscal year under subsection (b)(2); and
not increase or decrease the balance in the fund.
